title: AMBA Protocol Interview Question Bank
description: Reusable whiteboard, waveform triage, and verification-plan prompts for AHB, AXI, bridge, ACE, and CHI curriculum modules.

questions.prompt: A firmware-visible register block sits behind a high-throughput DMA fabric. Which AMBA protocols would you use on each side and why?
questions.lookFor: AXI for DMA/fabric throughput, APB for low-cost registers, bridge placement, latency and verification tradeoffs.

questions.prompt: When would an SoC team intentionally choose AHB instead of AXI?
questions.lookFor: Lower area/control complexity, in-order traffic, simple masters/slaves, lower outstanding transaction needs.

questions.prompt: Explain the difference between a protocol expressing intent and proving completion.
questions.lookFor: Address/control phase as intent, data/response as completion, and monitors that preserve transaction boundaries.

questions.prompt: How does receiver backpressure show up differently in AHB and AXI?
questions.lookFor: HREADY stalls the shared AHB pipeline; AXI READY applies independently per channel and can create per-channel congestion.

questions.prompt: Draw an AHB transfer with two wait states and explain which signals must remain stable.
questions.lookFor: Two-phase pipeline, HREADY low extending data phase, address/control stability, correct sample point.

questions.prompt: How do HTRANS, HBURST, and HSIZE combine to describe a burst?
questions.lookFor: NONSEQ start, SEQ continuation, bytes per beat, alignment, BUSY/IDLE distinction.

questions.prompt: An AHB slave waits for HADDR to change before releasing HREADY. Why can this deadlock?
questions.lookFor: Master holds address/control stable while HREADY is low, so the slave is waiting for an event the protocol forbids.

questions.prompt: What should a master and slave each do when an AHB transfer returns ERROR?
questions.lookFor: Legal response timing, no successful data commit, recovery to valid next transfer, scoreboard handling.

questions.prompt: Design an AHB monitor that records completed transactions under arbitrary wait states.
questions.lookFor: Accepted address/control queue, data-phase alignment, HREADY gating, response capture.

questions.prompt: What coverage model would you build for an AHB slave VIP?
questions.lookFor: Transfer type, size, burst, response, wait-depth, reset/error scenarios, cross coverage.

questions.prompt: Can AXI VALID depend on READY? Explain the rule and the failure mode.
questions.lookFor: VALID cannot wait for READY, payload stability until handshake, deadlock if both sides wait.

questions.prompt: A write never completes. Which AXI channels do you inspect first and why?
questions.lookFor: AW/W acceptance, WLAST, B response generation, independent channel stalls, outstanding counters.

questions.prompt: A 32-bit INCR burst starts at 0x0FF0 with AxLEN=7. Is it legal?
questions.lookFor: 8 beats, 4 bytes each, spans 0x0FF0 through 0x100F, crosses 4KB, must split.

questions.prompt: How do you compute the address sequence for an AXI WRAP burst?
questions.lookFor: Aligned wrap region, region size equals beats times bytes per beat, address wraps within region.

questions.prompt: Two reads use the same ARID and return out of issue order. Is that legal?
questions.lookFor: Not legal for same ID; per-ID ordering must be preserved.

questions.prompt: Sketch a scoreboard for out-of-order AXI reads.
questions.lookFor: Associative array by ID, ordered queue per ID, beat counters, RLAST and response checks.

questions.prompt: What events make an AXI exclusive write fail?
questions.lookFor: Conflicting write to monitored address, mismatched address/size/ID rules, response status handling.

questions.prompt: Which verification checks would you add for AxCACHE, AxPROT, and AxQOS?
questions.lookFor: Attribute propagation, security/privilege filtering, cacheability paths, QoS arbitration effects.

questions.prompt: Describe a circular backpressure deadlock in an AXI crossbar.
questions.lookFor: Blocked B/R path prevents W/AW/AR progress, finite buffers fill, no channel can drain.

questions.prompt: What failure can ID truncation introduce when multiple masters share a slave?
questions.lookFor: Ordering streams alias, responses return to wrong master or wrong transaction, scoreboard mismatch.

questions.prompt: How would you prove an AXI interconnect meets a throughput requirement?
questions.lookFor: Traffic model, channel utilization, stall attribution, outstanding depth, latency histogram, fairness.

questions.prompt: What checks catch master starvation under heavy AXI traffic?
questions.lookFor: Bounded grant latency, QoS-aware arbitration, long-run bandwidth distribution, random stall stress.

questions.prompt: Design the split logic for an AHB-to-AXI bridge when a burst crosses 4KB.
questions.lookFor: Bytes-to-boundary calculation, first and second AXI bursts, AxLEN encoding, data/response correlation.

questions.prompt: How should an AXI-side stall propagate back to an AHB master?
questions.lookFor: HREADY deassertion, source state stability, bounded buffering, response completion.

questions.prompt: Walk through a dirty cache-line read by another master in ACE.
questions.lookFor: Snoop request, cache response/data, ownership/state transition, memory update policy.

questions.prompt: Explain request, home, and subordinate node roles in CHI.
questions.lookFor: Requester intent, home node directory/coherency decisions, subordinate memory/device service.

questions.prompt: Whiteboard an AXI interconnect for four masters and two slaves.
questions.lookFor: Decode, arbitration, ID expansion, buffering, route tables, response demux, deadlock checks, verification plan.

questions.prompt: Given a stuck AXI waveform, describe your first five debug steps.
questions.lookFor: Identify stalled channel, check VALID/READY rules, outstanding counts, LAST, response routing, reset/error context.
